title: Africa's forgotten diseases: trial neglect analysis of 15 conditions with disproportionate African burden
primary_estimand: Neglect Index (Africa burden % / Africa trial share %) for each disease
certainty: high (registry data, complete enumeration within ClinicalTrials.gov)
body: Do Africa-endemic diseases receive clinical trial investment proportional to their burden on the continent? We searched ClinicalTrials.gov for interventional trials across 15 conditions that disproportionately affect Africa, including Burkitt lymphoma, Kaposi sarcoma, trypanosomiasis, schistosomiasis, onchocerciasis, trachoma, lymphatic filariasis, leishmaniasis, rabies, podoconiosis, mycetoma, noma, rheumatic heart disease, snakebite envenoming, and Buruli ulcer. For each disease we computed a Neglect Index defined as Africa burden share divided by Africa trial share. Several conditions had zero registered interventional trials in Africa, yielding an undefined Neglect Index. Rheumatic heart disease, which kills an estimated 240,000 Africans annually and is preventable with penicillin, and snakebite envenoming, designated a WHO neglected tropical disease in 2017, were among the most under-researched relative to their mortality. By contrast, high-income countries fund substantial rare-disease trial portfolios through orphan drug legislation. The analysis is limited to one registry and cannot capture unregistered community-level studies or trials in WHO or national databases.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.

checks.value: Burkitt, Kaposi, trypanosomiasis, schistosomiasis, onchocerciasis, trachoma, LF, leishmaniasis, rabies, podoconiosis, mycetoma, noma, RHD, snakebite, Buruli
Several diseases had zero Africa trials; mean NI >10x
RHD 240K deaths, snakebite WHO NTD 2017, HIC rare disease comparison
